ascii_border_rule: A loose 2-4 character wide ASCII decorative border is drawn on the outer artwork edge as a bottom underlayer before all text, generated inserts, gossip, captions, provenance strip, and frame graphics. It does not surround or label the Florentine source insert.
layering_rule: The Florentine source insert has no local label because the bottom strip now carries the base-character identity as DNA codon triplets plus serial. Legacy cube-world background text is replaced with textless UI/world shapes so no decorative layer can cross background words. Doodle underlay is shape-only; readable microtext is drawn later in foreground-safe boxes. Required plaques use measured non-overlap placement rather than falling back into collisions. The outer frame is an underlay, not a final overprint. Optional gossip/micro/style labels use compact text-fitted boxes, avoid reserved/source zones, and may be skipped rather than covering the insert. Source and Tarot objects now get soft focal halos, deckled tape/fiber tabs, and red-string evidence routes so the collage has depth and a clear reading path.
background_chatter_rule: Faded no-box conversational shade is drawn as palette/local-background contrast-aware text with a quiet 1px halo, then masked away from every reserved foreground/source box so it rewards zooming without competing with plaques/captions. Background chatter now uses compact complete marginalia-style fragments and trims without literal ellipses, so lines do not look accidentally cut off. Some lines gain broken/jittered marginal reply arrows, brackets, and underlines so the background chatter reads like annotated manuscript/forum evidence rather than clean vector UI.
background_texture_rule: A fine background-only duotone/newsprint tooth is drawn before source inserts and foreground text: sparse low-alpha dot screens plus tiny paper flecks, kept subtle so it adds print texture without dirtying labels or relics.
mystic_underlay_rule: Medici-era learned-magic atmosphere is added as an underlay: grunged stamps, seal diagrams, and handwritten marginalia referencing Ficino, the Corpus Hermeticum, the Key of Solomon, Hygromanteia, Greek/Hebrew/Arabic formula transmission, and the Laurenziana/Medici libraries; it is cleared from reserved foreground zones before text.
caption_symbol_rule: The main FLORENTINE BRAINROT caption box is now treated as the first-read object: captions are longer mystic/market ramblings, with subtle varied-size non-text stamped symbols drawn directly on the caption card behind the words. Each caption also gets a token-specific Tarot watermark in the safe lower/corner zone so the card trait affects the main story object, not just a separate mini-card.
background_symbol_rule: Background symbolism includes varied-size Hermetic/cabal-style seal circles, triangles, compass marks, eye/glyph centers, stamps, handwritten grunge, grimoire scratch lines, and panel-wide card-specific Tarot echoes, all under foreground-safe masking.
history_now_rule: HISTORY→NOW bridges now share a token-specific case file with captions, QR payloads, looksmax plaques, and background chatter. The old large banks are still used (64 old nodes × 66 modern nodes), but selections are independently deterministic and sentence-compatible instead of fragment-laced.
qr_code_rule: When QR boxes appear, they are real scannable QR codes with high-contrast modules and quiet zones. The encoded payload is plain text only: a short deterministic Nostradamus/Renaissance oracle message, not a URL, JSON blob, or metadata link. The QR matrix itself stays clean black-on-pale, while the surrounding container reads as a rough auction-evidence/parchment lot stamp with deckled borders, archive scuffs, and old-paper registration marks outside the quiet zone.
brainrot_text_rule: Visible copy uses a coherent text-thread system: 16 case labels, 12 evidence templates, 12 verdicts, and 16 priority chatter replies bind each token's source, Tarocchi card, history bridge, QR text, caption, and marginalia. Brainrot slang remains in secondary banks (131 terms, 55 creatures, 55 microtexts) but is no longer randomly bolted onto major text lanes.
looksmax_rule: Every source/base character receives one deterministic period looksmaxxing tip. The plaque now uses independent deterministic slot picks from 82 actions, 70 contexts, 74 results, and 8 evidence suffixes, avoiding adjacent copy-paste endings and random postscript lacing.
tarot_rule: The Tarocchi Fiorentini metadata trait uses the complete 97-card Tarocchi Fiorentini / Minchiate deck (41 trumps including Il Matto + 56 Latin-suit cards) with one deterministic shuffled card per design via seed 1492, stride 37, and offset 23. Across 1401 designs this yields 14 complete 97-card sets plus 43 extras; each rendered panel attempts a visible custom Tarocchi card graphic with the Italian card name/motif in the body and no redundant top trait label. Category-aware motifs cover Papi, virtues, elements, zodiac, Arie, suit pips, and court cards.
visible_label_rule: OpenSea-facing attributes stay marketplace-readable: Base, Panel Role, Palette, Style Tribe, Insert Mode, Generated Insert, Tarocchi Fiorentini, Gematria Word, Gematria Sum, Oracle when visible, Chaos Dial, and Brainrot Index. Marker/Serial are no longer public attributes; their codon/serial strings only remain if they are literally visible in the bottom provenance strip captured under florentine_brainrot.visible_text. Tarocchi Fiorentini is metadata-backed but the visible card relies on its Italian card name/motif rather than an extra top trait label. Output filenames stay zero-padded for sorting.
